description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a weathered, brown finish, showing signs of use and age. The axe head is made of metal, with a rectangular blade and a squared poll, attached securely to the top of the handle. The blade has a utilitarian, slightly rough surface, indicating it is designed for practical work rather than display. The transition between the handle and the blade is reinforced with a metal collar, providing additional durability. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A weathered hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a rectangular metal blade, reinforced by a metal collar. The design is utilitarian and shows signs of regular use.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top of the handle, showing the circular cross-section and the metal collar securing the blade.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the rectangular metal blade and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the bottom of the handle, highlighting the end grain of the wood and the metal collar above.
description_view_3: The opposite side view of the axe, again showing the blade profile and the straight wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, medium-brown, no visible grip features, round cross-section, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular single-edged blade with squared poll, no beard, utilitarian profile, rough finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal collar reinforcement.
